cards.title: Why Grandparents Lose Contact With Grandchildren
cards.body: Strong grandparent bonds grow from respect, trust, and care. Engaging in small, thoughtful moments today can prevent the kind of distance that breaks hearts later.

cards.title: Do Abused Parents Become Child Abusers?
cards.body: Abused children tend to repeat the same abusive behavior toward their children and partners. Many interacting factors increase risk for intergenerational maltreatment.

cards.title: Keeping Dead Wife's Books Safe for Our Son
cards.body: Reading with his son reminds a widowed father of the world of words his wife shared with him. It helps him let go of overwhelming guilt.

cards.title: Parent of Teenager Doing A-levels Feels Helpless
cards.body: Parents of teenagers face unique challenges they cannot control or fix. Holding flashcards for advanced exams fills some parents with a unique kind of horror.

cards.title: We Found Baby on Subway, Now He's 26
cards.body: A New York social worker discovered an abandoned infant on the subway in 2000 and became his father. The unexpected adoption transformed both their lives.

cards.title: Education Secretary Reviews Hidden Childcare Costs
cards.body: The education secretary is asking regulators to examine childcare costs families still face despite government funding. Many families report unexpected expenses beyond advertised rates.

cards.title: Family Estrangement Effects on Wellbeing Mixed
cards.body: Estrangement is not binary but a continuum of reducing contact with family members. Support plays an important role in wellbeing, whether or not people seek reconciliation.
